SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.flare

Class RelevantWindCalculator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.flare.RelevantWindCalculator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class RelevantWindCalculator extends Object implements Serializable
Relevant-wind calculator for flare thermal-radiation studies. Wind speed and direction strongly affect the location and magnitude of the peak radiation footprint of a flare: wind bends the flame, moving the radiation centre and the point of closest approach to people and equipment. Radiation studies therefore evaluate a "relevant" (design) wind speed and the worst wind sector rather than just a single calm case.

This utility:

  • scales a reference wind speed to the flare-tip elevation using the power-law wind profile V(z) = Vref · (z / zref)α;
  • scans a supplied wind rose (sectors with mean speed and frequency) for the worst (highest) sector wind at the flare elevation;
  • returns a frequency-weighted "relevant" wind speed, i.e. the speed whose cumulative exceedance frequency exceeds a chosen design fraction.
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim
See Also:

    Description
    void
    addSector(String direction, double speedAtReference, double frequency)
    Adds a wind-rose sector.

    calc()
    Runs the relevant-wind calculation: scales each sector to the flare elevation, finds the worst sector, and computes the frequency-weighted relevant wind speed.
    double

    Returns the frequency-weighted relevant (design) wind speed at the flare elevation.

    Returns the list of wind-rose sectors (with scaled speeds populated after calc()).

    Returns the direction label of the worst sector.

    Returns the worst (highest) sector wind speed at the flare elevation.

  • Constructor Details

    • RelevantWindCalculator

      public RelevantWindCalculator()
      Default constructor for RelevantWindCalculator.

  • Method Details

    • setProfile

      public void setProfile(double referenceHeightM, double flareTipElevationM, double shearExponent)
      Sets the elevation and wind-profile parameters.
      Parameters:
      referenceHeightM - reference measurement height in meters (must be > 0)
      flareTipElevationM - flare-tip elevation in meters (must be > 0)
      shearExponent - power-law wind-shear exponent (typically 0.10-0.30)

    • setDesignExceedanceFraction

      public void setDesignExceedanceFraction(double fraction)
      Sets the design cumulative-exceedance fraction used for the relevant wind speed.
      Parameters:
      fraction - cumulative-exceedance fraction (0-1)

    • addSector

      public void addSector(String direction, double speedAtReference, double frequency)
      Adds a wind-rose sector.
      Parameters:
      direction - sector direction label (e.g. "N")
      speedAtReference - mean wind speed at the reference height in m/s (must be ≥ 0)
      frequency - annual frequency of this sector (0-1)

    • scaleToFlare

      private double scaleToFlare(double speedAtReference)
      Scales a reference wind speed to the flare elevation using the power-law profile.
      Parameters:
      speedAtReference - wind speed at the reference height in m/s
      Returns:
      wind speed at the flare elevation in m/s

    • calc

      public void calc()
      Runs the relevant-wind calculation: scales each sector to the flare elevation, finds the worst sector, and computes the frequency-weighted relevant wind speed.

    • getWorstSectorSpeed

      public double getWorstSectorSpeed()
      Returns the worst (highest) sector wind speed at the flare elevation.
      Returns:
      worst sector wind speed in m/s

    • getWorstSectorDirection

      public String getWorstSectorDirection()
      Returns the direction label of the worst sector.
      Returns:
      worst sector direction label

    • getRelevantWindSpeed

      public double getRelevantWindSpeed()
      Returns the frequency-weighted relevant (design) wind speed at the flare elevation.
      Returns:
      relevant wind speed in m/s

    • getSectors

      public List<RelevantWindCalculator.WindSector> getSectors()
      Returns the list of wind-rose sectors (with scaled speeds populated after calc()).
      Returns:
      list of wind sectors

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serializes the calculation results to a pretty-printed JSON string.
      Returns:
      JSON representation of the results
